items.headline: Nigerian unions launch nationwide strike, shutting down the power grid.
items.body: Organized labor began an indefinite strike over the national minimum wage. This action immediately halted power and transport for millions of people.

items.headline: Argentina's Senate approves President Milei's sweeping state reform package.
items.body: The controversial bill passed after a tense and prolonged debate. The law now grants the executive broad new powers over the economy.

items.headline: Mexico's peso falls sharply after the ruling party's landslide election.
items.body: The currency dropped over four percent on fears of constitutional changes. This immediately increases the cost of imported goods for Mexican consumers.

items.headline: South Korea announces new cash payments to boost its birth rate.
items.body: The government is offering new financial incentives to parents. These policies aim to address the world's lowest fertility rate.

es.headline: Final passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship depart Spain.
es.body: Two planes evacuated the last travelers from the Canary Islands. This action ends a tense public health and diplomatic standoff.

no.headline: Norwegian banks raise mortgage rates, increasing costs for homeowners.
no.body: Several major banks increased their mortgage rates following a central bank hike. The move directly raises monthly housing costs for many Norwegian homeowners.

uk.headline: Nationalist party wins plurality in Welsh election, ending Labour's dominance.
uk.body: Plaid Cymru won the most seats in the Welsh Parliament election. This result ends a century of Labour party dominance in Wales.

nl.headline: Government says most businesses are unprepared for network failure
nl.body: Over half of Dutch firms lack plans for a major cyberattack or blackout. The government is now urging companies to create 72-hour emergency procedures.
